summary: Fire‑filled set with stellar Althea solo and tight Cold Rain & Snow
blurb: The show delivers an energetic first set highlighted by Althea, On The Road Again and a ripping Jack‑A‑Roe. The second set rolls through a soaring Cold Rain And Snow into a jam‑heavy Estimated Prophet/Eyes Of The World sequence, capped off by a surprise Satisfaction encore. Overall the performance is tight and vibrant, with Garcia often described as "on fire."
review: On November 26, 1980 at the Sportatorium, the Dead turned in an exhilarating night that still feels fresh to this day. The first set bursts open with the high‑octane “Alabama Getaway” and quickly settles into a series of standout moments – a soaring Althea solo that has fans calling it "epic," a gritty “On The Road Again,” and a rollicking Jack‑A‑Roe that leads into a tight “New Minglewood Blues.”  The second set is the real highlight: “Cold Rain And Snow” launches a fiery run through “Samson & Delilah,” then the classic “China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider” segue, before the band launches into an expansive “Estimated Prophet > Eyes Of The World” stretch that showcases Phil’s inventive space work and Mickey/Bill’s funky, complex drumming.  The night closes with a heartfelt “Wharf Rat,” a punchy “Good Lovin’,” and a jubilant Satisfaction encore that leaves the crowd buzzing.  Across all available recordings, listeners note Garcia’s tone as “on fire” throughout, Brent’s organ bursts adding rhythmic texture, and a rhythm section that drives the jams forward despite occasional mix quirks in the soundboard source.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five of six recordings are clearly positive, praising Althea, Cold Rain & Snow, Estimated Prophet, and Garcia’s "on fire" playing; only one source is mixed but still notes strong moments. Consistent language like "smoking," "blistering second set," and "stellar" across multiple analyses warrants a solid positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good‑quality soundboard capture with clear instrument separation; despite a drum‑heavy mix it preserves the show’s energy and includes all key moments.

key_highlights: Althea solo is highlighted as epic and soaring.
Cold Rain And Snow launches a high‑energy second set.
Estimated Prophet > Eyes Of The World jam showcases tight group interplay.
Garcia repeatedly described as "on fire" throughout the night.
song_highlights: It Must Have Been The Roses

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers energetic vocals and an epic Althea solo; his tone is repeatedly called "on fire," especially on On The Road Again, though the mix sometimes buries his guitar.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides strong, melodic bass work with a notable solo after the drums; his space‑work shines during the second‑set jams.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s rhythm playing is high‑energy and described as "awesome" throughout, driving the band forward.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland’s keyboards dominate the mix, adding quick organ bursts, bleats, and rhythmic complexity that sometimes eclipse other instruments but contribute to the show’s power.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s drumming is relentless and funky, often highlighted for its complex rhythms and driving presence in the mix.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann delivers a steady, powerful beat that locks in with Hart, forming a propulsive dual‑drum foundation.

must_listen_sequences: It Must Have Been The Roses
